title: Platinum, Aurora, And The Conformance Project
subtitle: A browser-arcade platform, evidence-led game ingestion program, and release discipline for AI-assisted software.
intent: Keep a public, talkable slide overview next to the white paper so the high-level project story, latest conformance data, ingestion process, release discipline, and forward plan stay visible during dev/beta/production review.

slides.title: Platinum, Aurora & The Conformance Project
slides.claim: A real game platform, a playable first game, and an evidence program for making AI-assisted software accountable.

slides.title: The Claim: Fast AI-Assisted Work Can Still Be Accountable
slides.claim: The project forces model-assisted iteration to leave evidence, checks, and public release artifacts behind.
proof: Local CPU/browser harnesses for repeated measurement

slides.title: The Arc Moved From One Game To A Conformance Program
slides.claim: Each release family made the project harder to fake and easier to review.

slides.title: The Operating Model Is Five Layers, Not One Blob
slides.claim: Quality improves when reference evidence, ingestion, games, harnesses, and release claims have separate ownership.

slides.title: Platinum Hosts; Game Packs Carry Game-Specific Authority
slides.claim: The platform scale bet only works if Aurora-specific truth does not leak into Platinum or the next game.
proof: Platform owns shell, services, and lane model
Architecture review says dev/beta separation is good but still worth hardening

slides.title: Current Game Portfolio: First Game, Second Proof, Third Intake
slides.claim: The platform matters because it can host genuinely different game instances, not just skins.
proof: Conformant reference modes first, unique public themes later

slides.title: Ingestion Turns Examples Into Implementation Contracts
slides.claim: External examples become source manifests, windows, annotations, runtime captures, scores, and release gates.

slides.title: The Reference Corpus Expanded Beyond One Remembered Target
slides.claim: Recent source recovery makes claims more defensible and exposes gaps more sharply.
proof: Galaga challenge videos, audio cues, sprite/walkthrough bundles
Cabinet, manual, rule, and history sources

slides.title: The Score Is A Measurement Surface, Not A Trophy
slides.claim: Scores carry confidence and resolution; a better scorer can lower a score while improving honesty.

slides.title: Aurora Is Playable And Much More Honest Than It Was
slides.claim: The candidate is strong enough for review, but not yet for an easy 1.4.1 production promise.
proof: Highest-value next gameplay step is movement grammar

slides.title: Challenge-Stage Work Improved The Process Before It Improved The Game
slides.claim: This is a measurement and ingestion success, but still a gameplay blocker.

slides.title: The Next Unlock Is A Reusable Movement Grammar
slides.claim: Challenge stages and normal-stage entries should use the same contract language for group arrivals, paths, timing, and player opportunity.

slides.title: Audio Is Valuable, But The Economics Tell Us To Be Disciplined
slides.claim: Audio clarity matters, but current tuning loops are expensive for modest score movement.
proof: Audio loop around 39 minutes per positive score point
Next gain should come from better evaluators or more stable cue windows

slides.title: Sprites Need Three Separate Conformance Concepts
slides.claim: One static sprite score hides the work players actually perceive.

slides.title: Personas Turn Gameplay Assessment Into Repeatable Evidence
slides.claim: Beginner, intermediate, professional, and expert runs help expose score, survival, and learnability changes.
proof: Distribution matters more than one run
Capture/rescue persona behavior still needs richer strategy

slides.title: The Economics Ledger Keeps Us Honest About Spend Versus Return
slides.claim: The project tracks CPU, browser, Codex/GPU-equivalent time, artifact growth, and score movement.

slides.title: Release Discipline Is Part Of The Product
slides.claim: The lane model prevents a passing build from becoming an exaggerated public promise.
proof: MacBook is release authority but production remains conservative

slides.title: Professionalism Also Means Review Records And Issue Closure
slides.claim: Code, architecture, documentation, security, and storage reviews are now artifacts rather than vibes.

slides.title: Next 90 Days: Make Quality Movement Visible And Release-Worthy
slides.claim: The near-term plan is quality conversion, not just more artifacts.
proof: 1.4.1 candidate only when improvements are player-visible
Production story only when release notes can defend the claim

slides.title: The Longer Goal: A Conformance Factory, Then Original Variations
slides.claim: First prove true-to-reference modes; then make public games unique with confidence.
proof: Publish an honest AI-assisted software story
